event: denied exposure to any other medications or chemicals
chest radiograph bilateral lower lobe infiltrates
transferred to the intensive care unit
transthoracic echocardiogram showed a large vegetation at the tip of anterior leaflet of tricuspid valve measuring 2 cm x 1.2 cm
transesophageal echocardiogram with contrast revealed patent foramen ovale with shunt
acute tubular necrosis secondary to perfusion-related kidney injury
CT chest showed multiple cavitary peripheral lung nodules
blood cultures grew methicillin sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA)
oxacillin (2 g IV every 6 h) treatment was initiated
12 days after initiation of oxacillin, bilateral nontender purpuric papules localized predominantly on the dorsal aspect of the hands and feet
lesions on the lateral aspect of thighs
ANA weakly positive at a titer of 1:40
no significant changes in red blood count
no significant changes in leukocyte count
no significant changes in kidney function
eosinophil count within the normal limits throughout the hospital stay
aspirated fluid from bullous lesions did not grow bacteria
no organisms seen on gram staining
punch biopsies showed perivascular neutrophil infiltration with fibrinoid necrosis of small vessels
no viral inclusions identified on biopsy
vancomycin was started in lieu of oxacillin
